title: Listening - Part 1
description: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two people, a man and a woman, discussing a new architectural project in their city.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I finally saw the plans for the new library complex. It’s certainly a departure from the traditional stone buildings in that part of town. || Woman: It’s bold, I’ll give it that. But [1]I can't help feeling that the sheer scale of the glass facade will overwhelm the surrounding square[/1]. It feels a bit like a giant mirror reflecting everything but having no character of its own. || Man: I see your point. Though the architects claim the transparency is meant to symbolise 'open access to knowledge'. || Woman: That sounds like marketing speak to me. [2]What really bothers me is the lack of green space in the final draft[/2]. They’ve replaced the original community garden with a concrete plaza that’ll be a heat trap in the summer. || Man: True, that’s a significant loss. I suppose they prioritised ease of maintenance over environmental benefits.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I finally saw the plans for the new library complex. It’s certainly a departure from the traditional stone buildings in that part of town.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It’s bold, I’ll give it that. But I can't help feeling that the sheer scale of the glass facade will overwhelm the surrounding square. It feels a bit like a giant mirror reflecting everything but having no character of its own.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I see your point. Though the architects claim the transparency is meant to symbolise 'open access to knowledge'.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That sounds like marketing speak to me. What really bothers me is the lack of green space in the final draft. They’ve replaced the original community garden with a concrete plaza that’ll be a heat trap in the summer.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: True, that’s a significant loss. I suppose they prioritised ease of maintenance over environmental benefits.

questions.question: What is the woman's opinion of the library's design?

options.A: It is too similar to other modern buildings.
options.C: It is inappropriately small for its location.

questions.explanation: B is correct as she describes it as 'having no character of its own' and just reflecting its surroundings [1].

questions.question: The woman is particularly unhappy about

options.A: the cost of the materials being used.
options.B: the removal of a local amenity.
options.C: the symbolic meaning of the architecture.

questions.explanation: B is supported by her concern about the 'lack of green space' and the replacement of the 'community garden' [2].

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues, a man and a woman, talking about a recent work trip to a trade fair.
extracts.audio_script: Man: That trade fair was exhausting. I think I’ve seen enough brochures to last me a lifetime. || Woman: It was intense. But [3]I found the networking event on the second night surprisingly productive[/3]. I managed to have a long chat with the head of procurement from that German firm we’ve been trying to land. || Man: That’s a result! I spent most of that night stuck in a corner discussing logistics software. || Woman: Well, it’s all about being in the right place at the right time. [4]My only complaint was the venue itself[/4]; the acoustics were so bad I could barely hear the keynote speakers, and the air conditioning was practically non-existent. || Man: I agree. I think they tried to pack too many exhibitors into a space that wasn't designed for it. It felt quite claustrophobic at times.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That trade fair was exhausting. I think I’ve seen enough brochures to last me a lifetime.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It was intense. But I found the networking event on the second night surprisingly productive. I managed to have a long chat with the head of procurement from that German firm we’ve been trying to land.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That’s a result! I spent most of that night stuck in a corner discussing logistics software.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Well, it’s all about being in the right place at the right time. My only complaint was the venue itself; the acoustics were so bad I could barely hear the keynote speakers, and the air conditioning was practically non-existent.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. I think they tried to pack too many exhibitors into a space that wasn't designed for it. It felt quite claustrophobic at times.

questions.question: The woman feels that the trip was worthwhile because

options.A: she learned about new industry trends.
options.B: she made a valuable business connection.
options.C: she was able to distribute a lot of promotional material.

questions.explanation: B is correct because she mentions having a productive chat with the 'head of procurement' from a target firm [3].

questions.question: What did the speakers agree was a problem at the event?
questions.explanation: B is supported by their complaints about the 'venue itself', the 'acoustics', and it being 'claustrophobic' [4].

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new documentary about the history of the internet.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Did you see that documentary on the origins of the web? I thought the archive footage from the nineties was brilliant. || Woman: It was nostalgic. [5]What I appreciated most was how it highlighted the collaborative spirit of the early pioneers[/5]. It wasn't just about the technology; it was about the ideal of free information for everyone. || Man: True, it’s a far cry from the commercialised platform it’s become today. || Woman: [6]I just felt the final segment on future trends was a bit pessimistic[/6]. They focused almost entirely on data privacy and cybercrime, without mentioning any of the potential for positive social change. || Man: I see where you’re coming from, but I suppose those are the most pressing issues right now. It was a bit of a wake-up call, if anything.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Did you see that documentary on the origins of the web? I thought the archive footage from the nineties was brilliant.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It was nostalgic. What I appreciated most was how it highlighted the collaborative spirit of the early pioneers. It wasn't just about the technology; it was about the ideal of free information for everyone.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: True, it’s a far cry from the commercialised platform it’s become today.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I just felt the final segment on future trends was a bit pessimistic. They focused almost entirely on data privacy and cybercrime, without mentioning any of the potential for positive social change.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I see where you’re coming from, but I suppose those are the most pressing issues right now. It was a bit of a wake-up call, if anything.

questions.question: What did the woman find most impressive about the documentary?

options.A: the quality of the historical research
options.B: the focus on the human cooperation involved
options.C: the explanation of the technological breakthroughs

questions.explanation: B is correct as she highlights the 'collaborative spirit of the early pioneers' [5].

questions.question: What was the woman’s reaction to the end of the film?

options.C: She thought it was overly optimistic.

questions.explanation: A is supported by her comment that the final segment was 'pessimistic' and focused only on negative aspects like cybercrime [6].
